skill.name: codex-pet
skill.description: Generate Codex Pet spritesheet and manifest files from one reference image using RunComfy and ImageMagick.
skill.summary: Create a Codex-compatible custom pet from a reference image.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static findings are mostly expected for a documentation skill that provides shell commands for RunComfy, ImageMagick, and Codex pet installation. No malicious prompt injection or credential exfiltration was found, but the recipes use network calls, local file writes, and an unsanitized pet name in paths.

medium_findings.title: Unsanitized Pet Name Used In File Paths
medium_findings.description: PET_NAME is used directly to construct RUN_DIR and DEST. A value containing path traversal can write generated files outside the intended pet folders.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The variable is quoted, which reduces shell injection risk, but it is not validated before path construction. The write operations are explicit and target user-controlled paths.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Processing Of User Image URLs
medium_findings.description: The workflow sends a user-provided source image URL to the RunComfy GPT Image endpoint. This is expected behavior, but users should understand the privacy boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The RunComfy CLI call is central to the skill and the security notes disclose the outbound endpoints. The risk is disclosure of submitted image URLs and image content to a third party.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Recipes Require Local Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to install and run npm, runcomfy, and magick commands. These commands are legitimate for the workflow but expand the local execution surface.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are documented recipes rather than hidden code. They still require trusted tools and should be reviewed before execution in sensitive environments.

low_findings.title: Credential Access Is Disclosed And Limited To RunComfy Token Use
low_findings.description: The skill references RUNCOMFY_TOKEN and RunComfy token storage. This is required for authentication and no unrelated secret harvesting was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token references are specific to RunComfy authentication and the document states local token storage details. I found no evidence of sending unrelated environment secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static cryptography alerts appear to match dimensions, colors, hashes, or animation values in documentation, not cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains no implemented cryptographic algorithm. The flagged lines describe image generation, metadata, colors, or file content examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Command To RunComfy Model API
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented runcomfy command sends prompt JSON and a source image URL to an external model service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and CLI action are explicit in the workflow. This is intended, but it is a real outbound network dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Pet Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented install step writes generated files into CODEX_HOME or $HOME/.codex under a pet-specific folder.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands create a destination directory and copy generated artifacts into it. This is expected behavior but should be constrained to safe pet names.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution In Recipe
dangerous_patterns.description: The recipe uses command substitution to select the first generated PNG file from the decoded output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The substitution operates on a generated directory path and is quoted elsewhere. It is a normal shell pattern, but depends on predictable file names.

content.value_statement: Custom Codex pets need a precise sprite atlas and manifest. This skill guides RunComfy and ImageMagick to create those files from one reference image.
actual_capabilities: Defines the Codex Pet atlas size, frame grid, and animation row contract.
Provides a RunComfy CLI prompt for generating one canonical pet image.
Shows ImageMagick commands for chroma-key removal and cell normalization.
Builds nine animation rows through deterministic micro-transforms.
Writes a pet.json manifest and installs the finished files under CODEX_HOME.
limitations: Requires RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and ImageMagick installed locally.
Sends the source image URL to RunComfy for model processing.
Creates subtle motion from one pose, not fully distinct animation frames.
Pet names should be sanitized before running the install commands.

use_cases.title: Personalize Codex Desktop
use_cases.description: Create a custom companion that matches a user avatar, mascot, or favorite object.

use_cases.title: Prepare Contest Entries
use_cases.description: Generate compliant Codex Pet assets with the expected grid, frame counts, and manifest.

use_cases.title: Batch Internal Mascots
use_cases.description: Adapt a folder of approved reference images into installable Codex Pet packages.

prompt_templates.title: Make One Pet
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one reference image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Codex Pet from this image URL. Use the pet name team-helper and install it under my Codex pets folder.

prompt_templates.title: Control The Look
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Codex Pet from this product mascot. Keep the blue glasses, orange scarf, and friendly expression. Avoid backgrounds and text.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Animation
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants adjusted motion after a first pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Regenerate the sprite rows using the existing canonical image. Make the jump higher and the review head tilt more visible.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Pet Packages
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for several approved inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Codex Pet packages for these three source image URLs. Use safe kebab-case names and keep each run in a separate output folder.

output_examples.input: A user provides a logo image URL and the name docs-helper.
output_examples.output: A Codex Pet folder named docs-helper containing a manifest and a 1536 by 1872 WebP sprite atlas.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a stronger idle animation on an existing pet.
output: Updated row transforms for a more visible idle bob.
A rebuilt sprite atlas using the same canonical pet image.

output_examples.input: A team provides several approved mascot images for internal use.
output_examples.output: Separate installable pet packages with consistent style, frame layout, and Codex-compatible manifests.

best_practices: Use safe kebab-case pet names before running file creation or install commands.
Use public image URLs only when the source image is approved for third-party processing.
Review generated sprites before copying them into the Codex pets directory.
anti_patterns: Do not use private or sensitive image URLs as source inputs.
Do not accept pet names containing slashes, dot segments, or shell metacharacters.
Do not run the install commands before confirming CODEX_HOME points to the intended location.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Codex-compatible pet manifest and sprite atlas from one reference image.

faq.question: Does it require Codex Pro image generation?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI and a RunComfy token instead.

faq.question: What local tools are required?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI, ImageMagick, and access to a public source image URL.

faq.question: Where are generated pets installed?
faq.answer: The recipe copies pet.json and spritesheet.webp into CODEX_HOME or $HOME/.codex under pets.

faq.question: Is there a security warning?
faq.answer: Yes. Review the shell commands, sanitize pet names, and avoid private image URLs.

faq.question: Can the animation be customized?
faq.answer: Yes. The ImageMagick transform values can be adjusted for stronger or subtler motion.
